title: Spice Up Your January 12!
story: Every January 12, food lovers across America celebrate National Curried Chicken Day by cooking up this flavorful dish! Restaurants offer special curry deals, and families try new recipes with different spices like turmeric, cumin, and garam masala. Some people make mild curry for beginners, while others go super spicy. Food bloggers share their favorite curry photos online, and cooking classes teach kids how to blend spices safely. It's the perfect winter day to warm up with something delicious and exotic!
activity_question: What spices are commonly used in curried chicken?
bonus_fact: Curry powder was actually invented in Britain, not India!

title: Sip, Sip, Hooray for Hot Tea Day!
story: Tea lovers everywhere celebrate National Hot Tea Day on January 12 by brewing their favorite cups of steamy goodness! People share photos of colorful tea collections, try new flavors like chai or Earl Grey, and cozy up with warm mugs. Tea shops offer special deals and tastings. Some families make it extra fun by hosting tea parties with fancy cups and cookies, turning this simple drink into a delicious celebration.
activity_question: What do tea shops often offer on National Hot Tea Day?
follow_up_question: What's your favorite type of tea or what would you add to make tea time more fun?

title: Kiss a Ginger Day: Red Hair, Don't Care!
story: People with red hair get extra love on January 12 during Kiss a Ginger Day! This silly holiday celebrates redheads and their awesome fiery locks. Friends and family give hugs, high-fives, and yes, kisses to their favorite gingers. Social media fills up with photos of people showing off their natural red hair or temporary red hair spray. Some schools even have "wear red" days to join the fun. It's a sweet way to celebrate something that makes people unique and special!
activity_question: What do people often wear or use to join in Kiss a Ginger Day celebrations?
bonus_fact: Only about 2% of people worldwide are born with natural red hair!

title: Sweet Almond Dreams on Marzipan Day
story: Candy makers across the country roll out their sweetest creations every January 12 for National Marzipan Day! This amazing treat is made from ground almonds and sugar, creating a smooth paste that can be shaped into tiny fruits, animals, or flowers. Bakeries showcase colorful marzipan sculptures in their windows, while families try making their own at home. Some people eat it plain, others dip it in chocolate, and creative bakers use it to decorate cakes and cookies.
activity_question: What are the two main ingredients used to make marzipan?
follow_up_question: What shape would you create if you had marzipan to sculpt with?
